{"key_event":"1941 occupation and post-Reza Shah political opening","reasoning":"Reza Shah’s coercive centralization was sharply broken by Allied occupation, foreign penetration, regional revolts, and economic crisis, though the army, monarchy, territorial control, and planning apparatus had partly recovered by 1949. Civil society rose from severe repression to a much freer arena of parties, unions, newspapers, Majlis assertion, labor strikes, and nationalist mobilization, but the 1949 ban on Tudeh and expanded royal powers kept it only moderately strong.","society_change":3.0,"society_power":5.8,"state_change":-2.2,"state_power":5.6}
